title: FINRA Case 24-02632 — Justin Rees v. Robert W. Baird & Co. Incorporated
text:
Expungement Award
FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
In the Matter of the Arbitration Between:
Claimant Case Number: 24-02632
Justin David Rees
vs.
Respondent Hearing Site: Tampa, Florida
Robert W. Baird & Co. Inc.
Awards are rendered by independent arbitrators who are appointed pursuant to Rule 13806 of
the Code of Arbitration Procedure (“Code”) to issue final, binding decisions. FINRA makes
available an arbitration forum—pursuant to rules approved by the SEC—but has no part in
deciding the award.
Nature of the Dispute: Associated Person vs. Member
The expungement hearing was conducted by videoconference.
REPRESENTATION OF PARTIES
For Claimant Justin David Rees (“Claimant”): Chelsea Bauer, Esq., HLBS Law, Broomfield,
Colorado.
For Respondent Robert W. Baird & Co. Inc. (“Respondent”): Todd A. Holleman, Esq., Miller
Johnson, Detriot, Michigan and and Nicole D. Blanchard, Esq., Miller Johnson, Grand Rapids,
Michigan.
CASE INFORMATION
Statement of Claim filed on or about: December 16, 2024.
Claimant signed the Submission Agreement: December 16, 2024.
Statement of Answer filed on or about: February 6, 2025.
Respondent signed the Submission Agreement: February 6, 2025.
CASE SUMMARY
In the Statement of Claim, Claimant asserted a claim seeking expungement of customer dispute
information from registration records maintained by the Central Registration Depository (“CRD”).
In the Statement of Answer, Respondent did not oppose Claimant’s expungement request.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 24-02632
Award Page 2 of 5
RELIEF REQUESTED
In the Statement of Claim, Claimant (CRD Number 5570895) requested expungement of all
references to Occurrence Number 1862797 from the CRD registration records and any and all
other relief that the Panel deems just and equitable.
In the Statement of Answer, Respondent opposed any request for damages or other relief
against Respondent as unsupported by the allegations and claims.
OTHER ISSUES CONSIDERED AND DECIDED
The Arbitrators acknowledge that they have each read the pleadings and other materials filed by
the parties.
On December 18, 2024, Claimant advised that the customer in Occurrence Number 1862797
(“Customer”) was served with a copy of the Statement of Claim within 10 days of filing the
Statement of Claim. On December 19, 2024, Claimant filed an Affidavit of Service and provided
proof of service and proof of delivery.
On February 17, 2025, Claimant advised that the Customer was served with a copy of the
Statement of Answer within 10 days of its filing and provided proof of service and proof of
delivery.
In the Initial Prehearing Conference Order dated March 19, 2025, the Panel raised the question
whether, on the facts of this case, this claim was eligible for submission in light of FINRA Rule
13206(a) of the Code and directed a briefing on the issue. On June 20, 2025, Claimant filed a
Response to the Panel’s Sua Sponte Request on the applicability of FINRA Rule 13206 to the
eligibility of this claim for expungement, to which no response was filed. On July 1, 2025, the
Panel heard oral arguments on the issue. In an Order dated the same day, the Panel ruled as
follows: “On the facts of this case, in light of the alleged continuing injury to Claimant, FINRA
Rule 13206(a) does not prohibit submission of this claim.”
The Panel conducted a recorded hearing by videoconference on January 20, 2026, so the parties
could present oral argument and evidence on Claimant’s request for expungement. Claimant
appeared by videoconference at the expungement hearing. Respondent appeared at the
expungement hearing and, as stated in the Statement of Answer, did not oppose the request for
expungement.
The Customer did not appear at the expungement hearing.
No authorized representative of a state securities regulator appeared at the expungement hearing.
The Panel noted Claimant’s representation that a prior arbitration panel or court has not
previously ruled on expungement of the same occurrence in the CRD.
The Panel noted that the dispute related to Occurrence Number 1862797 was not settled.
Therefore, there was no settlement documentation to review.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 24-02632
Award Page 3 of 5
The Panel relied upon the following documentary or other evidence: Claimant’s testimony,
Exhibit 3, and Exhibit 7.
AWARD
After considering the pleadings, the testimony and evidence presented at the expungement
hearing, and any post-hearing submissions, the Panel has decided in full and final resolution of
the issues submitted for determination as follows:
1. The Panel awards the expungement of all references to Occurrence Number 1862797 from
registration records maintained by the CRD for Claimant Justin David Rees (CRD Number
5570895) with the understanding that, pursuant to Rule 2080, Claimant Justin David Rees
must obtain confirmation from a court of competent jurisdiction before the CRD will execute
the expungement directive.
Unless specifically waived in writing by FINRA, parties seeking judicial confirmation of an
arbitration award containing customer dispute expungement relief must name FINRA as an
additional party and serve FINRA with all appropriate documents.
Pursuant to Rule 13805 of the Code, the Panel has made the following affirmative finding of
fact:
The claim, allegation, or information is false.
The Panel has made the above finding based on the following reasons:
Although it is true that there were no transactions during the 2014-2015 time period
(other than dividend reinvestments), and that Respondent continued to charge an
advisory fee, nonetheless, a 1% asset based fee is industry standard and was
contractually bound. There is some evidence in the case that Claimant was
attentive to the needs of the Customer during the relevant time period. The fees
charged were therefore justified.
2. Any and all claims for relief not specifically addressed herein are denied.
FEES
Pursuant to the Code, the following fees are assessed:
Filing Fees
FINRA Dispute Resolution Services assessed a filing fee* for each claim:
Initial Claim Filing Fee =$ 1,600.00
*The filing fee is made up of a non-refundable and a refundable portion.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 24-02632
Award Page 4 of 5
Member Fees
Member fees are assessed to each member firm that is a party in these proceedings or to the
member firm(s) that employed the associated person(s) at the time of the event(s) giving rise to
the dispute. Accordingly, as a party, Respondent is assessed the following:
Member Surcharge =$ 2,000.00
Member Process Fee =$ 3,850.00
Postponement Fees
Postponements granted during these proceedings for which fees were assessed or waived:
October 6, 2025, postponement requested by the parties =$ 1,150.00
Total Postponement Fees =$ 1,150.00
The Panel has assessed the total postponement fees to Claimant.
Hearing Session Fees and Assessments
The Panel has assessed hearing session fees for each session conducted. A session is any
meeting between the parties and the Arbitrator(s), including a pre-hearing conference with the
Arbitrator(s), which lasts four (4) hours or less. Fees associated with these proceedings are:
Three (3) pre-hearing sessions with the Panel @ $1,150.00/session =$ 3,450.00
Pre-Hearing Conferences: March 17, 2025 1 session
July 1, 2025 1 session
November 18, 2025 1 session
One (1) hearing session on expungement request @ $1,150.00/session =$ 1,150.00
Hearing: January 20, 2026 1 session
Total Hearing Session Fees =$ 4,600.00
The Panel has assessed the total hearing session fees to Claimant.
All balances are payable to FINRA Dispute Resolution Services and are due upon receipt.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 24-02632
Award Page 5 of 5
ARBITRATION PANEL
F. Guthrie Castle, Jr. - Public Arbitrator, Presiding Chairperson
Adam Michael Porter - Public Arbitrator
Peter M. Cosel - Public Arbitrator
I, the undersigned Arbitrator, do hereby affirm that I am the individual described herein and who
executed this instrument, which is my award.
